name: Feature request
about: A new tendril for the garden
title: '[feature] '
labels: ['enhancement', 'triage']
assignees: []
---

<!--
Welcome to the Loci garden. Feature requests are how the garden learns
what it wants to grow next. Take the minute to frame it well.

This template asks the questions the Rainbow Zoku charter would ask
of any new direction: what is the user-facing problem, what would
change, and which of the four commitments is this aligned with.
-->

## The problem

<!-- 1-3 sentences. What can a user not do today, or what works poorly? -->

## Proposed direction

<!-- Sketch the shape of the answer. Concrete enough to argue about, not so concrete that it forecloses alternatives. -->

## What this serves

<!-- Tick the commitments this work serves. -->

- [ ] **Cognitive sovereignty** (the user's thinking, context, memory)
- [ ] **Digital dignity** (treating the user as a person, not a target)
- [ ] **Network equality** (participation without legibility)
- [ ] **Digital self-defense** (refuse, hide, leave, take the work with you)
- [ ] None of the above; it is good for other reasons (explain below)

## What this would NOT do

<!--
Important. A bounded feature is easier to ship and easier to refuse
scope creep on. What is explicitly out of scope of this request?
-->

## Alternatives considered

<!-- Optional. If you considered other shapes, name them and why this one wins. -->

## Provenance

- [ ] human-authored
- [ ] agent-authored-human-reviewed
- [ ] agent-authored-human-cosigned

Requester handle: <!-- pseudonym is fine -->
